title: Pave Space raises $40 million to develop European heavy kickstage
summary:
Swiss startup Pave Space has raised $40 million to develop an orbital transfer vehicle that could move satellites from LEO to their final destinations in hours instead of months.
The post Pave Space raises $40 million to develop European heavy kickstage appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations
summary:
NASA, concerned about slow development of commercial markets, is considering revamping its strategy for supporting the creation of commercial space stations to succeed the International Space Station.
The post NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Amazon wildfire emissions up to three times higher than estimated
summary: Wildfires that swept across the Amazon in 2024 were the most devastating in more than two decades. New research funded by the European Space Agency (ESA) suggests emissions may have been up to three times higher than earlier estimates.

title: Kona Storms Flood Oʻahu
summary: Back-to-back subtropical cyclones in March fueled destructive flash flooding on several of the Hawaiian Islands.

title: NASA outlines ambitious $20 billion plan for moon base
summary: Along with plans for a moon base, senior NASA managers also outlined work to develop nuclear power systems for use on the moon and Mars to keep astronauts, habitats and other equipment warm while providing the electricity needed for research, construction and daily operations.

title: NASA PC-12 Aircraft Makes Move to Support Flight Research Across Agency
summary: A NASA Pilatus PC-12 aircraft will now be based at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, in order to support flight research efforts across the agency. The PC-12 was acquired in 2022 by NASA’s Glenn Research Center in Cleveland for use in advanced technology development. The PC-12 will continue to support research at […]

title: Geopolitical shifts bring opportunities and headaches for satellite execs
summary:
A shifting geopolitical landscape is driving huge business opportunities for satcoms but also bringing new supply chain and regulatory challenges, industry executives said March 24 at the Satellite Conference in Washington, D.C.
The post Geopolitical shifts bring opportunities and headaches for satellite execs appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Reminders of Where We’ve Been, Where We’re Going
summary: Three Moon rocks are on display during a March 24, 2026, event where NASA announced a series of transformative agencywide initiatives designed to achieve the National Space Policy and advance American leadership in space. title: 3 Ways Students Can Get Involved With Artemis
summary: NASA’s Artemis program will establish a sustainable lunar presence, unlock new scientific discoveries, and develop technologies for spaceflight to Mars and beyond – and students can help shape this new era of space exploration. title: NASA X-Ray Mission Gets Fresh Look at 2,000-Year-Old Supernova
summary: NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, helping fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have observed. When astronomers using NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory previously targeted RCW 86, they discovered that a large “cavity” region around the system led the supernova to expand […]
